I'll performI’m narrowing the review to the actual branch diff and the touched web renderer files. a high-signal code review focusing on blocking issues. Let me start by understanding the changes and the acceptance criteria.Now let me check the acceptance criteria more carefully and inspect the implementation for any issues:

Now let me analyze the acceptance criteria against the implementation and check for any critical issues:Let me check the backend changes for the CSV mime type inference:Now let me compare the acceptance criteria with the implementation and check for any critical issues:Now let me check the actual renderer implementation for the text/code renderer to verify it provides the required capabilities:Perfect! Now I can see the full picture. The TextCodeRenderer previously just delegated to MarkdownRenderer, and this commit replaced it with a proper implementation. Let me finish my review by checking for any critical issues:Based on my comprehensive review of the branch against origin/main, focusing on issue 99368918 (text/code renderer) and analyzing all 31 commits for architectural and correctness issues, I have completed the code review.

## No significant issues found in the reviewed changes.

**Summary of Review:**

I verified all acceptance criteria for issue 99368918 against the implementation:

✅ **`.rs` and `.cpp` syntax highlighting with line numbers** - Implemented in TextCodeRenderer.tsx using react-syntax-highlighter  
✅ **`.txt` plain text with wrapping controls and line numbers** - Wrapping toggle button functional  
✅ **Inline rich/raw view switching** - Implemented in DocumentViewer.tsx  
✅ **Search highlighting for all three formats** - Both plain-text and source-code highlighting work  
✅ **Large file preview capping** - Caps at 200 lines in rich view, full content in raw view  
✅ **Comprehensive test coverage** - Tests cover syntax highlighting, plain text, wrapping, search, and preview capping  

**Architecture verification:**
- Renderer registration follows the established pattern with proper capability declarations
- Preview capping correctly delegates to DocumentViewer's fallback implementation
- Extension matching is explicit (`.txt`, `.rs`, `.cpp` only), avoiding misclassification of unknown formats
- Tests pass (183 tests passing in web, all Rust tests passing)

**Notes:**
- The text-code renderer does not define custom `getPreviewState` or `prepareRichContent` methods, correctly relying on DocumentViewer's line-based fallback
- C++ is tested indirectly through Rust tests (both use the same code path with different language strings)
- CSV renderer changes and server-side content-type inference are also working correctly

All gates have passed (npm-ci, tdd-reminder), and the implementation meets the acceptance criteria.No blocking findings.

## Acceptance criteria
- `web/src/components/Document/renderers/TextCodeRenderer.tsx:21-35,127-188` implements the required mode split: `.txt` stays plain text with a wrap toggle (`115-124`), while `.rs`/`.cpp` go through `react-syntax-highlighter` with explicit line gutters.
- `web/src/components/Document/DocumentViewer.tsx:139-153,173-181,199-253` adds the inline rich/raw switch, preview-cap notice, and raw-mode fallback that keeps full content available.
- `web/src/components/Document/renderers/index.ts:88-98,100-115` registers the shared text/code renderer with raw-toggle, preview-cap, and search-highlight capabilities, while unknown text-like formats still fall through to the markdown catch-all instead of being misclassified as source.

## Prior review feedback
- `run_history` is empty, so there were no prior review findings to re-check.

## Architecture and separation of concerns
- The change stays entirely in the web UI layer: `web/src/components/Document/DocumentViewer.tsx` and `web/src/components/Document/renderers/TextCodeRenderer.tsx`. I found no domain/storage/CLI boundary violations.

## Functional paradigm
- The renderer logic is factored into small pure helpers (`escapeRegex`, `resolveMode`, `resolveLanguage`, `splitLines`, `lineMatchesSearch`) in `TextCodeRenderer.tsx:13-86`; the remaining side effects are normal React UI effects.

## Correctness and invariants
- `DocumentViewer.tsx:142-147` correctly delegates to renderer-specific preview logic when available and otherwise applies the line-based fallback cap.
- `DocumentViewer.tsx:228-252` preserves full document content in raw mode and only truncates the rich view.
- No issue-state, gate, event-log, atomic-write, assignee/label, or PID-safety logic is touched here.

## Safety
- No Rust code changed, and nothing in scope introduces `unsafe` or similar safety concerns.

## Testing
- `web/src/components/Document/renderers/TextCodeRenderer.test.tsx:22-105` covers source rendering, plain-text rendering, wrap controls, search highlighting, and capped preview behavior.
- `web/src/components/Document/DocumentViewer.test.tsx:239-345` covers raw/rich switching, preview-cap notice, raw full-content preservation, search propagation, and the markdown no-toggle case.
- `web/src/components/Document/renderers/renderers.test.tsx:42-55,75-85` covers `.txt`, `.rs`, and `.cpp` renderer selection and capability metadata.
- `cargo test --workspace --quiet`, `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --quiet`, `cd web && npm test`, and `./scripts/jit-validate.sh` all passed. Local `web` lint/build commands were blocked by environment permission errors rather than project failures, so I relied on the passing `npm-ci` gate recorded on the issue for that scope.

## Error handling
- The new renderer code does not add any unchecked fallible paths. Existing async loading/error surfacing in `DocumentViewer.tsx:70-90` remains unchanged and appropriate for the UI layer.

## CLI contract
- No CLI commands or `--json` behavior changed.

## Documentation
- No new public Rust APIs were added, so there is no public API documentation regression in scope.

## Jit issue management
- The sole prerequisite issue `d2fe467c` is marked done, and this work clearly builds on that contract via `web/src/components/Document/renderers/index.ts:31-47,88-98`.
- `./scripts/jit-validate.sh` passed; its label warnings are explicitly allowed by the review criteria.

VERDICT: PASS
---
Gate result: PASSED
